name: SUPERIOR MESENTERIC ARTERY SYNDROME
definition: ARTERIOMESENTERIC DUODENAL COMPRESSION SYNDROME = WILKIE SYNDROME = CHRONIC rare cause of duodenal obstruction ← compression of 3rd portion of duodenum between SMA
dominant_finding: reduced distance between SMA + aorta to 2-8 mm (AMD)
discriminating_features: narrowing of angle between SMA + aorta to 6-22° (AMA)
reduced distance between SMA + aorta to 2-8 mm (AMD)
megaduodenum = pronounced dilatation of 1st + 2nd portion of duodenum + frequently
vertical linear compression defect in transverse portion of duodenum overlying spine
abrupt change in caliber distal to compression defect

general: narrowing of angle between SMA + aorta to 6-22° (AMA)
reduced distance between SMA + aorta to 2-8 mm (AMD)
megaduodenum = pronounced dilatation of 1st + 2nd portion of duodenum + frequently
vertical linear compression defect in transverse portion of duodenum overlying spine
abrupt change in caliber distal to compression defect
relief of obstruction by postural change into prone knee-elbow position
